name: Colorblind Safe
description: High-contrast palette designed for deuteranopia, protanopia, and tritanopia. Avoids red-green conflicts and uses perceptually distinct colors across all common forms of color vision deficiency.

accessibility_notes.palette_source: Based on Wong (2011) colorblind-safe palette and IBM Design Language color-blind safe palette
accessibility_notes.deuteranopia: No red-green pairs used adjacently; blue and orange provide primary contrast
accessibility_notes.protanopia: Warm tones use orange (#D55E00) and yellow (#E69F00) instead of red; cool tones use distinct blues
accessibility_notes.tritanopia: Avoids blue-yellow confusion by ensuring sufficient luminance contrast between all blue and yellow-family colors
